lede: A look at September 22 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Michael Faraday and Ben Chifley, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Sara Jane Moore
events.desc: Sara Jane Moore tries to assassinate U.S. President Gerald Ford, but is foiled by the Secret Service.

events.title: Mali
events.desc: The Sudanese Republic is renamed Mali after the withdrawal of Senegal from the Mali Federation.

events.title: Haiti
events.desc: In Haiti, François Duvalier is elected president.

events.title: Gail Halvorsen
events.desc: Gail Halvorsen officially starts parachuting candy to children as part of the Berlin Airlift.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: A joint German–Soviet military parade in Brest-Litovsk is held to celebrate the successful invasion of Poland.

events.title: Gresford disaster
events.desc: The Gresford disaster in Wales kills 266 miners and rescuers.

events.title: Queen Victoria
events.desc: Queen Victoria surpasses her grandfather King George III as the longest reigning monarch in British history (later surpassed by her great-great-granddaughter Elizabeth II on 9 September 2015).

events.title: Joseph Smith
events.desc: Joseph Smith claims to have found the golden plates after being directed by God through the Angel Moroni to the place where they were buried.

events.title: United States Postmaster General
events.desc: The office of United States Postmaster General is established.

events.title: Eighty Years' War
events.desc: Eighty Years' War: A Spanish force led by the Marquis del Vasto successfully fights its way past a joint English/Dutch ambush in the Battle of Zutphen.

births.name: Ben Chifley
births.line: Australian engineer and politician, 16th Prime Minister of Australia

deaths.text: Paul van Zeeland, Belgian lawyer, economist, and politician, 38th Prime Minister of Belgium
Emperor Zhaozong of Tang, emperor of the Tang Dynasty

observances.text: Christian feast days: Ignatius of Santhià (Lorenzo Maurizio Belvisotti)
